content.full_text:
September 7, 1976

Mr. Alexander Babin, Treasurer
Walnut Creek Democratic Club
50 Kevin Court
Walnut Creek, California 94596

Dear Mr. Babin:

As you requested in your letter of August 23, 1976, and our telephone conversation on September 2, 1976, I am enclosing information regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

You stated that the Walnut Creek Democratic Club has never had a bank balance above $250 and has never contributed candidates. You said further that funds collected are used to fi-nance newsletters to your members, social affairs and non-parti-san voter registration drives. As I explained, a political club has no reporting obligations under the Act until it has collected $500 or more in a calendar year earmarked for the making of con-tributions or expenditures to influence the action of voters.

If you have any questions about the enclosed material, please contact me.

Sincerely,


Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

ALEXANDER BABIN
50 KEVIN COURT
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 94596
PHONE (415) 934-3267

August 23, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Gentlemen:

I am the new treasurer of the Walnut Creek Democratic Club. I was elected to that position on January 16, 1976.

It was just brought to my attention that no Association Campaign Statement - Short Form - has been filed since July 31, 1975. Since I am not familiar with the exact reporting requirements, I would appreciate receiving a copy of your: "Information Memoranda on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" as well as: "Record-keeping Guidelines for Campaign Committees and Treasurers" including: "Regulation on Mass Mailings".

I would also appreciate receiving copies of whatever forms I am supposed to file with you.

Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours

Alexander Babin

Alexander Babin, Treasurer,
Walnut Creek Democratic Club


P.S. This letter is the result of a visit today by:
Mr. Michael A. Rand, Political Reform Auditor
Franchise Tax Board, Suite 418
1390 Market Street
San Francisco, Ca. 94102

[illegible handwritten notes at bottom left]

[The document is mostly illegible due to poor image quality and fading. Most of the text appears as very faint or washed out marks that cannot be reliably transcribed. There appears to be some handwritten signatures or marks in the middle-lower portion of the page, and what looks like a date stamp or marking on the left side near the bottom, but the specific text is not legible enough to transcribe with confidence.]

You stated that the Walnut Creek Democratic Club has never had a bank balance above $250 and has never contributed to candidates. You said further that funds collected are used to finance newsletters to your members, social affairs and non-partisan voter registration drives. As I explained, a political club has no reporting obligations under the Act until it has collected $500 or more in a calendar year earmarked for the making of contributions or expenditures to influence the action of voters. If you have any questions about the enclosed material, please contact me. Sincerely, Nan Hambleton Special Compliance Representative NH:bw Enclosures bcc: Jeanne P. Dear Mr. Replin: I apologize for expending the valuable resources of your organization forwarding the attached reference materials. As you requested in your letter of August 23, 1976, and our telephone conversation on September 5, 1976, I am enclosing Mr. Alexander Replin's resume.
